summary: Recent heavy-ion-relevant arXiv activity centered on QCD phase structure, small-system femtoscopy, antinucleus coalescence phenomenology, and physics-aware AI/ML. The strongest scientific signals were a chemically extended critical-surface construction, a lattice benchmark of three-flavor QCD with Mobius domain-wall fermions, and ALICE pp femtoscopy with upgraded-detector data. The most transferable ML papers used physics structure explicitly: symmetry-informed nuclear mass models, neural-network TMD extraction, spectral-function collider event representations, and detector-oriented generative modeling.
strongest_paper_summary: The most compelling set combines arXiv:2606.28282 for finite-density QCD critical-surface mapping, arXiv:2606.28098 for ALICE femtoscopy source-size measurements, and arXiv:2606.28287 for physics-constrained interpretable nuclear mass modeling.

papers.title: QCD critical surface from constant entropy contours
papers.authors: Hitansh Shah, Tristan Gyure, Anabella Leon, Francesco Di Clemente, Mauricio Hippert, Claudia Ratti, Volodymyr Vovchenko
papers.abstract: Maps a proposed QCD critical surface in full (T, mu_B, mu_Q, mu_S) space using an expansion along constant-entropy contours. Finds a heavy-ion-relevant strangeness-neutral shift of the critical baryochemical potential by roughly 40-100 MeV with little change in critical temperature.

papers.title: The QCD phase diagram for three-flavor Mobius domain-wall fermions
papers.authors: Yu Zhang, Yasumichi Aoki, Jishnu Goswami, Shoji Hashimoto, Issaku Kanamori, Takashi Kaneko, Yoshifumi Nakamura
papers.abstract: Lattice-QCD study of the three-degenerate-flavor transition at zero baryon chemical potential using Mobius domain-wall fermions. Finite-size behavior at the studied mass points is consistent with crossover rather than first- or second-order transition.

papers.title: Multiplicity dependence of the size of the common hadron emission source in pp collisions at the LHC
papers.abstract: ALICE measures proton-proton correlations in 13.6 TeV pp collisions using 2022 upgraded-detector data. The analysis extracts femtoscopic source radii versus multiplicity and transverse mass and compares pp behavior with Pb-Pb source-radius trends.

papers.title: Antideuteron production from beauty-hadron decays: a first phenomenological study
papers.authors: Marta Razza, Nicolo Jacazio, Francesca Bellini
papers.abstract: Estimates antideuteron production from anti-Lambda_b and B- decays using PYTHIA and quantum-mechanical coalescence with an Argonne-v18 wave function. Provides first branching-ratio ranges and ALICE-accessible yield targets.

papers.title: Bridging Ab Initio Symmetries and Global Nuclear Masses with Interpretable Neural Networks
papers.authors: Phong Dang, Evander Espinoza, Xiaoliang Wan, Michela Negro, Jerry P. Draayer, Feng Pan, Tomas Dytrych, Daniel Langr, David Kekejian
papers.abstract: Constructs neural-network and compact nuclear-mass models from SU(3) and SU(4) Casimir operators. Trained on AME2016 and validated on AME2020-new nuclei, with a compact Wigner-informed model reporting 0.430 MeV validation RMSE.

papers.title: Neural-Network extraction of TMDs with SIDIS data
papers.abstract: Reports a neural-network unpolarized TMD extraction combining Drell-Yan and SIDIS data at N^3LL accuracy. Including SIDIS broadens extracted TMD PDFs and reduces uncertainties relative to DY-only fits.

papers.title: Machine learning fully hadronic events with spectral functions
papers.authors: Mohammad Mahdi Altakach, Hadi Hassan, Sabine Kraml, Kazuki Sakurai, Haitham Zaraket
papers.abstract: Uses two-point correlation spectral functions as fixed-length, permutation-safe features for variable-multiplicity fully hadronic events. In a gluino benchmark, the method improves projected mass reach versus an ATLAS analysis and kinematics-only neural network.

papers.title: Qudit extension of parameterized IQP circuits: A generative quantum machine learning approach to integer data
papers.authors: Robert J. Banks, Arianna Crippa, Matthias Traube, Josua Unger, Christian Ertler, Wolfgang Lechner
papers.abstract: Extends parameterized IQP circuits toward integer-valued non-binary data and validates on CLIC electromagnetic-calorimeter electron-shower deposits. The method aims to preserve metric structure lost in binary encodings.

papers.title: Recovering Sharp Conductivity Features in the Finite-Data Calderon Problem with Physics-Informed Neural Networks
papers.authors: Ali AlHadi Kalout, Pablo Tejerina-Perez, Konstantin Karchev, Pedro Tarancon-Alvarez, Leonid Sarieddine, Raul Jimenez, Max Engelstein, Guy David
papers.abstract: Studies PINN-based finite-data inverse reconstruction with multiscale boundary excitations and Fourier-feature encodings. Reports synthetic reconstruction errors of roughly 3-12 percent across sharp and smooth conductivity fields.

papers.title: Analysis of the hidden-charm pentaquark candidates in the J/psi Xi* mass spectrum via the QCD sum rules
papers.abstract: Hadron-spectroscopy paper flagged on the arXiv listing with an admin note for text overlap with earlier arXiv papers. Not central to today's heavy-ion or AI/ML target scope.
